plan.executiveSummary: For schema-path testing only, STR-BALANCED is the sole deterministic option that projects at least 95% protected revenue inside the $420,000 boundary.
plan.strategyRationale: The simulated plan projects 96.4% protected revenue for $386,000 in 18 days, but quality, customs, labor, compressor, and contingency assumptions still require independent challenge and human authority.
evidenceCitations: EVD-QLT-115 requires fresh alternate-supplier batch validation.
EVD-LOG-117 identifies customs dependence for interplant transfer.
EVD-LAB-112 records unconfirmed weekend labor capacity.
EVD-SUP-116 records unresolved compressor supply risk.

assumptions.statement: Fresh batch validation completes within 36 hours.
Interplant transfer clears customs on the primary route.
Compressor supply remains sufficient through Day 21.

crossTeamPriorities.dependency: Plant COO approval and CFO-delegate authority.

crossTeamPriorities.priority: Validate and commit bounded Redwood Alloys capacity.
Validate the C-17 sample without changing NX-400 thresholds.
Resequence Line C by customer criticality.
Dispatch interplant inventory and reserve limited priority freight.
crossTeamPriorities.dependency: Finance authorization, customs contingency, and supplier commitment.
Updated dates; external sending remains unavailable.

residualRisks: The supplier may miss the 36-hour quality window.
Customs may delay the interplant transfer.
A compressor constraint could reduce protected revenue below 95%.

findings.title: Quality timing is not yet authorization
findings.detail: Supplier commitment must remain blocked until the fresh sample passes unchanged NX-400 thresholds.

findings.title: Customs creates a transfer dependency
findings.detail: The interplant transfer needs a documented contingency before dispatch.

findings.title: Weekend labor remains unconfirmed
findings.detail: Production resequencing must wait for bounded labor confirmation.

findings.title: Compressor risk reduces plan margin
findings.detail: A daily monitor and human re-escalation condition are required.

findings.title: Contingency requires separate authority
findings.detail: The $35,000 reserve must remain locked behind CFO-delegate approval.

unsupportedClaims: Quality release will complete within 36 hours.
The primary customs route will clear without delay.
Weekend labor will remain available through execution.
requiredConditions: Validate quality release before supplier commitment.
Validate the customs contingency before inventory transfer.
Confirm labor availability before production resequencing.
Keep the $35,000 reserve locked behind CFO-delegate approval.
Monitor compressor risk daily and pause for human re-escalation on material change.

counterfactual.scenario: Compressor supply tightens while customs clearance adds two days.
counterfactual.expectedImpact: Protected revenue could fall to 94.7%, requiring human revision instead of silently expanding scope.
